info.title: Delphi Markets Parlays API
info.description: Kalshi RFQ (Request for Quote) and MVE (Multi-Value Event) parlay trade data, plus bulk data export endpoints.

tags.name: Parlays
tags.description: Kalshi RFQ (Request for Quote) and MVE (Multi-Value Event) parlay trade data. Includes individual RFQ lookups, parlay trade history, and bulk data export in Parquet/JSONL format.

get.description:
Retrieve all Request for Quote (RFQ) events for a given RFQ UUID. Returns the full lifecycle of an RFQ (created, quoted, deleted, etc.).

## What you get
- RFQ lifecycle events (created, deleted, etc.)
- Creator and quote creator identifiers
- Associated market and event tickers
- Full JSON message with RFQ details (target cost, timestamps)

## Use cases
- Track the lifecycle of a specific RFQ
- Analyze RFQ creation and deletion patterns
- Monitor institutional quoting activity

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/klsi/rfq/822dcdee-e5c1-47da-9ddf-15287fb37c44`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

Retrieve all Request for Quote (RFQ) events for a specific date. Returns every RFQ that was created on that day across all markets.

## What you get
- All RFQ lifecycle events for the given date
- Creator and quote creator identifiers
- Associated market and event tickers
- Full JSON message with RFQ details

## Use cases
- Analyze daily RFQ volume
- Monitor institutional quoting activity over time
- Track block trade demand by day

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/klsi/rfq/date/2026-02-13`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

Download RFQ or MVE trades data for a specific date as presigned S3 URLs. Returns a finalized daily Parquet file if the day is complete, or live hourly JSONL parts if the day is still in progress.

Requires a Max or Admin plan.

## What you get

- Presigned S3 download URLs (valid for 5 minutes)
- Automatic detection of finalized vs live data
- Full daily Parquet file for completed days
- Hourly JSONL parts for the current day

## Use cases

- Bulk download historical data for backtesting
- Build local analytics pipelines
- Feed data into Pandas, Polars, or DuckDB
- Stream today's data hour-by-hour for near-real-time analytics

## Datasets

- rfq: Kalshi Request for Quote events (same schema as the /klsi/{id}/rfq endpoint)
- mve_trades: Kalshi Multi-Value Event parlay trades (same schema as the /klsi/{id}/mve_trades endpoint)

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/data/rfq?date=2026-02-25`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today (UTC).

parameters.name: hour
parameters.description: Hour filter (00-23). Only applies to live (non-finalized) days. Returns only parts for that specific hour.

200.description: Data export with presigned download URLs

403.description: Forbidden - requires Max or Admin plan

get.description:
Convenience endpoint that returns today's data (UTC). Equivalent to calling the date endpoint with today's date. Returns live hourly JSONL parts since the day is typically still in progress.

Requires a Max or Admin plan.

## What you get

- Today's data as presigned S3 download URLs
- Hourly JSONL parts accumulated so far today
- Optional hour filter to get a specific hour's data

## Use cases

- Quick access to today's data without specifying a date
- Monitor today's RFQ or MVE activity in near-real-time
- Feed into streaming analytics pipelines

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/data/rfq/latest`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

parameters.name: hour
parameters.description: Hour filter (00-23). Returns only parts for that specific hour of today.

200.description: Data export with presigned download URLs

403.description: Forbidden - requires Max or Admin plan

get.description:
Retrieve all Request for Quote (RFQ) events for a given Kalshi market ticker. Returns every RFQ that was created for this market.

## What you get
- All RFQ events for the market
- RFQ lifecycle events (created, deleted, etc.)
- Creator and quote creator identifiers
- Full JSON message with RFQ details (target cost, timestamps)

## Use cases
- See all RFQ activity on a specific market
- Analyze demand for block trades
- Monitor institutional interest in a market

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/klsi/KXATPMATCH-26FEB13FRIKOR-FRI/rfq`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

Retrieve all Multi-Value Event (MVE) parlay trades for a given Kalshi market ticker. MVE trades are multi-leg bets that combine multiple markets.

## What you get
- Trade timestamps and prices
- Taker side and contract count
- MVE collection and event tickers
- Individual leg details (market tickers, event tickers, sides)
- Number of legs in each parlay

## Use cases
- Track parlay trading activity on a market
- Analyze multi-leg bet structures
- Monitor institutional parlay flow

## Example

- Request: `GET /api/v1/klsi/KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S202613EAE13A377-ED7156B203E/mve_trades`
- Header: `X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

RFQRow.description: A Request for Quote (RFQ) event from Kalshi. Each row represents a lifecycle event (e.g., rfq_created, rfq_deleted) for a specific RFQ.

msg.description: JSON message containing RFQ details (target_cost, timestamps, etc.)

DataExportResponse.description: Response containing presigned S3 download URLs for a dataset export. The status field indicates whether this is a finalized daily Parquet or live hourly JSONL parts.

status.description: Whether the data is finalized (full daily Parquet) or live (hourly JSONL parts)

format.description: File format of the downloadable parts

hour.description: Hour filter applied (only present when filtering live data by hour)

parts.description: List of downloadable file parts with presigned URLs

MVETrade.description: A Multi-Value Event (MVE) parlay trade from Kalshi. Each row represents a single parlay trade with multiple legs.

leg_market_tickers.description: Market tickers for each leg of the parlay

leg_event_tickers.description: Event tickers for each leg of the parlay

leg_sides.description: Side (yes/no) for each leg of the parlay

num_legs.description: Number of legs in the parlay

DataExportPart.description: A single downloadable file part with a presigned S3 URL.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return (default: 10 in docs, max: 10000). Use a higher limit in your code.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Your API key (get one from the test-key endpoint above)
